title: println - printing in Java
author: Prodipta Acharjee
concept: Java prints with System.out.println, which writes its argument and then a newline.
explanation: Everything in Java lives in a class, and printing goes through System.out. println prints one value and moves to the next line; + joins text and a number into one string.

exercises.question: What does this print?
hints: + joins the text and the number.

exercises.question:
What does this print?

System.out.println(1 + 2);
hints: Here both sides of + are numbers, not text.

exercises.question: When one side of + is text, what does + do?
hints: It glues the number onto the text.
It joins them into one string.
